headline: Early days of war against Iran boost satisfaction in Israel
subhead: Israel has continued to bombard Iran with massive, unrelenting waves of military aircraft, while also attacking Hezbollah in Lebanon. Its missile defense system has helped limit the damage on Israeli territory.

headline: US says it is nearing 'complete control' of Iran's airspace
subhead: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the US and Israel 'will have complete control of Iranian skies, uncontested airspace.' Trump rated the US performance in the war as a '15' out of 10, while the US Senate rejected a resolution aimed at curbing Trump's authority to continue military strikes on Iran.

headline: Sri Lanka recovers 87 bodies from Iranian warship sunk off its coast by a US submarine
subhead: The Iranian vessel was the country's 'prize ship,' according to the US defense secretary, while the top US officer said the US has destroyed more than 20 Iranian navy vessels and neutralized 'Iran's major naval presence in theater.'

headline: Lebanon's displaced have a bitter feeling of déjà vu
subhead: The Lebanese government has reported that more than 58,000 people have fled their homes since the latest war between Israel and Hezbollah broke out.

headline: With the Strait of Hormuz blocked, regional trade disruptions could ripple across the globe
subhead: Following the US-Israeli strikes on Iran, maritime traffic in the Persian Gulf has ground to a halt. These disruptions risk causing congestion in Asian ports.

subhead: The Bourse de Commerce unveils 'Clair-obscur,' an exhibition featuring around 20 artists who explore the space between shadow and light.

headline: Iran's neighbors, under drone attacks, confront their vulnerability
subhead: Lacking experience in countering kamikaze drones, especially the infamous Shahed models, the Gulf countries have shown how unprepared they were for the threat posed by Iran, which is determined to exploit their vulnerability.

headline: 'A war justified once again by a lie turns into a race against the clock for Donald Trump'
subhead: The third American war waged in the Middle East by a president of the United States bears little resemblance to the previous ones, observes Le Monde's columnist Gilles Paris.
